summary: Add --concurrency flag for parallel iteration execution using Promise.race pool with no external dependencies
context: Benchmark runs take 59-125 hours sequentially. Each iteration creates an isolated working directory, so they are independently parallelizable. Weekly token quota (~848M) is the binding constraint, not per-minute rate limits — the run sustained ~12 sessions/hour for 69 hours with only sporadic transient failures.
rationale: Simple Promise.race concurrency pool in the orchestrator loop. At concurrency=1, behavior is identical to the previous sequential for loop. At concurrency=2, wall time halves to ~29 hours. No external dependencies (p-limit, p-queue) — the pool is 8 lines of code. Resume support preserved: completed iteration keys are tracked and skipped regardless of execution order. State persistence (collector.savePartialRunState) is serialized by the JS event loop since each processIteration awaits its own persistence calls.

alternatives.option: Use p-limit or p-queue npm package
alternatives.reason_rejected: Adding a dependency for 8 lines of concurrency control is unnecessary. The Promise.race pool pattern is well-understood and has no edge cases for this use case.

alternatives.option: Parallelize at the session level within an iteration
alternatives.reason_rejected: Sessions within an iteration are sequential by design — Agent B needs Agent A's output. Iteration-level parallelism is the right granularity.
